findings.evidence: `rg` shows the new `elementaryTwoQuotient : ModN (Additive G) 2` at `TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:52`, while the existing `SquareClassGroup : Additive Kˣ ⧸ (Subgroup.square Kˣ).toAddSubgroup` and `squareClass` remain at `TauCeti/FieldTheory/SquareClassGroup.lean:35` and `:46`.
findings.fix: Refactor `TauCeti.FieldTheory.SquareClassGroup` so `SquareClassGroup K` and `squareClass` are aliases/specializations of `elementaryTwoQuotient Kˣ` and `elementaryTwoQuotientMk`, then derive the square-class lemmas from the generic ones.
findings.issue: `elementaryTwoQuotient` duplicates the existing field square-class construction rather than making it the general API; `TauCeti.SquareClassGroup K` remains a separate quotient by `Subgroup.square Kˣ`, so users get two parallel APIs for `Kˣ/(Kˣ)^2`.

summary: The generic quotient is at the right mathematical level, but it is added alongside an existing special-case square-class quotient instead of subsuming it, leaving duplicate non-defeq APIs for the same object.
